---
title: "title_block"
---

[< Back to Reference](../reference.qmd) | [View source](https://codeberg.org/usrbinr/stwd/src/branch/main/R/stwd.R#L567)

# `title_block()`

Create a Title Block Plot

## Usage

```r
title_block(
  title,
  title_size = 12,
  halign = "left",
  lineheight = 1.1,
  margin_top = 5,
  margin_bottom = 5,
  margin_left = 5,
  margin_right = 5,
  width = 0.95,
  wrap_width = NULL,
  ...
)
```

## Arguments

| Argument | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| `title` | Main title text (marquee-formatted). Supports markdown formatting: **bold**, *italic*, {#color text. |
| `title_size` | Size for title in pts. Default: 16 (matches SWD style). |
| `halign` | Horizontal alignment: "left", "center", or "right". Default: "left". |
| `lineheight` | Line height multiplier. Default: 1.1. |
| `margin_top` | Top margin in pts. Default: 10. |
| `margin_bottom` | Bottom margin in pts. Default: 15. |
| `margin_left` | Left margin in pts. Default: 5. |
| `margin_right` | Right margin in pts. Default: 5. |
| `width` | Maximum width for text wrapping (0-1). Default: 0.95. |
| `wrap_width` | Character width to wrap text at. Default: NULL (no wrapping). |
| `...` | Additional arguments passed to `marquee::geom_marquee()`. |

## Value

A ggplot object that can be combined with other plots via patchwork.

## Description

Creates a standalone title element as a ggplot, for use as the
top element in a patchwork composition. Allows for larger, more
prominent titles than standard ggplot titles, matching the SWD style
where titles are bold statements that draw the eye.

## Examples

```r
## Not run: 
library(patchwork)

title_plot <- title_block(
    "**Q4 Sales: {#E63946 Apples} Take the Lead**",
    title_size = 18
)

title_plot / my_chart + plot_layout(heights = c(0.08, 0.92))

## End(Not run)
```
